symbols.name: s_feas
symbols.def: s_feas = the analysis clip exponent optimizing the def:feasible-rate balance g (q_n = n^{-s_feas}).

symbols.name: q_n
symbols.def: Lower-bound weak-arm scale: q_0 if gamma=0 else h_n^{beta_ag}.

symbols.name: M_n
symbols.def: Minimax regret inf_{pi_hat} sup_{P} E_P R_P(pi_hat).

symbols.name: U_n
symbols.def: Feasible upper risk sup_P E_P R_P(pi_hat_n).

symbols.name: B_n
symbols.def: Active block of the two-point witness.

symbols.name: qbar_n
symbols.def: Feasible clip: 1/4 if beta_ag=0 else hbar_n^{beta_ag}.

symbols.name: L_n
symbols.def: Logarithmic design scale (e.g. log n) controlling cell count and per-cell sampling.

assumptions.condition: O_1, ..., O_n are i.i.d. draws from P.
P(0 < |tau_P(X)| <= u) <= C_m u^alpha for all 0 < u <= u_0, with the Tsybakov decision-margin parameter domain alpha >= 0, C_m > 0, u_0 > 0 (intrinsic to the named condition).
Either P_X{tau_P(X)=0}=0, or every pi in Pi agrees with pi_star P_X-almost everywhere on {tau_P(X)=0}.
P{p_P(X) <= v, 0 < |tau_P(X)| <= u} <= C_o u^alpha v^(1/gamma) for all 0 < u <= u_0 and 0 < v <= c_o u^gamma, with v^(1/gamma)=1 when gamma=0.

novel.justification: The joint margin/one-sided-overlap envelope couples the Tsybakov margin exponent alpha to the polynomial overlap-decay exponent gamma in a single tail bound. It is the class-defining condition whose induced minimax exponent r_*(alpha,gamma) is the paper's contribution; it strictly generalises the strict-overlap (gamma=0) margin class and the constant-margin overlap-decay class.

assumptions._orchestrator_note: JSON-consistency fix (2026-06-24): added u<=u_0 to match ass:margin's range; the witness construction (tau_0>u_0) and the witness-membership proof both require u<=u_0. The original text omitted it, making the condition strictly stronger than ass:margin and unsatisfiable by the two-point witness off-block.

assumptions.condition: Pi is a pointwise measurable class of measurable deterministic policies with finite VC dimension d_Pi < infinity: there is a countable subclass Pi_0 subset Pi such that every pi in Pi is the pointwise limit of a sequence from Pi_0.
pi_star is an element of Pi for every law P in the class.

For the pointwise measurable finite-VC policy class Pi, every centered policy-indexed process with envelope B and conditional second moment bounded by C B^2 P_X(D_pi) has fixed-radius localized supremum bounded by C B n^{-1/2} r^{alpha/(2+2 alpha)}(log n)^p under thm:margin-localization.
The cross-fitted nuisance estimators satisfy ||muhat_a-mu_a||_{L2(P)} <= r_mu_n and ||ehat-e||_{L2(P)} <= r_e_n for a in {0,1}, with product-rate r_mu_n r_e_n = O(n^{-1/2}).
When gamma=0, p_P(X) >= underline_p holds P_X-almost surely, for a constant underline_p in (0,1/2].
The cross-fitted outcome-regression estimates muhat_0, muhat_1 take values in [-1,1].
There exist a >= 0, c >= 1/2 and constants C_mu, C_prod such that r_mu_n <= C_mu n^(-a) and r_mu_n r_e_n <= C_prod n^(-c) for all large n.
The number K of cross-fitting folds in def:feasible-erm is a fixed finite integer independent of n, and the deterministic fold partition is balanced as stated there.

For the same pointwise measurable finite-VC policy class Pi, every centered policy-indexed empirical process z_pi=(P_n-P)g_pi built from an i.i.d. sample of size n, with envelope B and conditional second moment bounded by C B^2 P_X(D_pi), satisfies E sup_{pi in Pi}{2|z_pi|-R_P(pi)/4}_+ <= C (B^2/n)^{A_alpha}(log n)^p.

definitions.name: R_P
definitions.construction: V_P(pi) = E_P[pi(X) tau_P(X)]; pi_star(x) = 1{tau_P(x) >= 0}; R_P(pi) = V_P(pi_star) - V_P(pi).

definitions.name: (beta_ag, D_ag, r_star)
definitions.construction: beta_ag = (0 if gamma = 0 else alpha gamma/(alpha+1)); D_ag = 2 + alpha + beta_ag; r_star(alpha,gamma) = (1+alpha)/D_ag.

definitions.name: (s_feas, r_feas)
definitions.construction: For one fixed nuisance regime (a,c,C_mu,C_prod), let A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0, fix u_bar in (0,u_0] and q_0 in (0,min{1/2,c_o u_bar^gamma}]. Define phi(s,t)=min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)+alpha t/2, 2a-t} on the compact feasible set 0<=s<=1/2 and 0<=t<=s/gamma. Let (s_feas,t_feas) be any maximizer, set g_joint=phi(s_feas,t_feas), q_n=q_0 n^{-s_feas}, and u_n=u_bar n^{-t_feas}. Then q_n<=c_o u_n^gamma for all large n, and the solved conditional feasible upper exponent is r_up=r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)}. For gamma=0, use fixed q_n=q_0<=underline_p/2 and set r_up=r_feas=min{A_alpha,c}.

definitions.name: e_q
definitions.construction: e_q(x; eta) = min{1-q, max{q, e(x)}} for clip q in (0,1/2] and nuisance eta=(mu_0,mu_1,e).

definitions.name: Gamma_q
definitions.construction: Gamma_q(O; eta) = mu_1(X) - mu_0(X) + (A/e_q(X;eta))(Y - mu_1(X)) - ((1-A)/(1-e_q(X;eta)))(Y - mu_0(X)).

definitions.name: pi_hat_n
definitions.construction: Fix a countable pointwise-dense subclass Pi_0=(pi_j)_{j>=1} from ass:policy-class and a deterministic balanced K-fold partition I_1,...,I_K of {1,...,n}, where each fold has size floor(n/K) or ceil(n/K). With foldwise cross-fitted nuisances etahat^{(-k)} and clip q in (0,1/2], define Vhat_{n,q}(pi)=n^{-1} sum_i pi(X_i) Gamma_q(O_i; etahat^{(-k(i))}), where k(i) is the evaluation fold of observation i. Let j_n be the smallest index j such that Vhat_{n,q}(pi_j) >= sup_k Vhat_{n,q}(pi_k) - 1/n, and set pi_hat_n=pi_{j_n}. This measurable Pi-valued 1/n-ERM is the feasible clipped-AIPW ERM. The clip q is the analysis tuning parameter chosen by the bias-variance balance.

definitions.name: M_n
definitions.construction: M_n(alpha,gamma) = inf over measurable data-dependent Pi-valued estimators pi_hat of sup_{P in P_class} E_P R_P(pi_hat).

definitions.name: U_n
definitions.construction: Fix one nuisance regime (a,c,C_mu,C_prod) and the deterministic schedule (q_n,u_n) selected by def:feasible-rate for that regime. Then U_n(alpha,gamma,a,c; etahat) = sup E_P R_P(pi_hat_n), where the supremum ranges over P in def:law-class for which ass:optimal-in-class holds, over supplied cross-fitted nuisance estimators satisfying ass:nuisance-rate, ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, and ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents with those same exponents a,c and regime constants C_mu,C_prod, with Pi satisfying ass:policy-class, ass:vc-localized-envelope, and ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope, and with the cross-fitting scheme satisfying ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count.

definitions.name: P_class
definitions.construction: { P : ass:bounded-outcome, ass:positivity, ass:margin, ass:zero-effect, ass:overlap-decay, and ass:strict-overlap-endpoint hold at exponents (alpha,gamma) }

definitions.name: (P_{n,+}, P_{n,-})
definitions.construction: On X=[0,1] with P_X=Lebesgue, fix h_n=n^{-1/(2+alpha+beta_ag)}, q_n=(1/4 if beta_ag=0 else h_n^{beta_ag}), and an active block B_n=[0, c_B h_n^alpha] with P_X(B_n)=c_B h_n^alpha. Choose tau_0 in (u_0,2). The two laws P_{n,sigma}, sigma in {+,-}, share P_X, share the logging propensity e(x)=q_n on B_n and e(x)=1/2 off B_n, and share the off-block outcome law supported on {-1,1} with E[Y|X=x,A=1]=tau_0/2 and E[Y|X=x,A=0]=-tau_0/2 for x notin B_n, so the common off-block optimal label is 1 and the common off-block contrast is tau_0. On B_n set Y=0 almost surely when A=0, and on the active treated cell {X in B_n, A=1} let Y be supported on {-1,1} with P(Y=1 | X in B_n, A=1)=(1+sigma h_n)/2 and P(Y=-1 | X in B_n, A=1)=(1-sigma h_n)/2, so E[Y|X in B_n,A=1]=sigma h_n and tau_{P_{n,sigma}}=sigma h_n on B_n. The laws agree everywhere off the treated active cell. Constants are chosen with 8 C_B C_Q < log 5.

statements.statement: Under ass:bounded-outcome, for every deterministic pi in Pi, R_P(pi) = E_P[ |tau_P(X)| 1{pi(X) != pi_star(X)} ] = integral over X of |tau_P(x)| 1{pi(x) != pi_star(x)} dP_X(x).
statements.justification: Regret equals the |contrast|-weighted disagreement mass; the exponent-free identity underlying margin localization and the converse separation.
statements.proof_tex:
Let \(\pi\in\Pi\) be deterministic. By the definition of welfare regret,
\[
R_P(\pi)=V_P(\pi_\star)-V_P(\pi)
=E_P\{(\pi_\star(X)-\pi(X))\tau_P(X)\}.
\]
The bounded-outcome assumption gives \(\mu_a(X)\in[-1,1]\), hence \(\tau_P\in[-2,2]\), so the expectation is finite. Pointwise in \(x\), if \(\tau_P(x)>0\) then \(\pi_\star(x)=1\) and
\[(\pi_\star(x)-\pi(x))\tau_P(x)=\tau_P(x){\bf 1}\{\pi(x)=0\}=|\tau_P(x)|{\bf 1}\{\pi(x)\ne\pi_\star(x)\}.
\]
If \(\tau_P(x)<0\), then \(\pi_\star(x)=0\) and
\[(\pi_\star(x)-\pi(x))\tau_P(x)=-\pi(x)\tau_P(x)=|\tau_P(x)|{\bf 1}\{\pi(x)\ne\pi_\star(x)\}.
\]
If \(\tau_P(x)=0\), both sides are zero, independently of the tie convention in \(\pi_\star\). Therefore
\[
(\pi_\star(X)-\pi(X))\tau_P(X)=|\tau_P(X)|{\bf 1}\{\pi(X)\ne\pi_\star(X)\}
\quad P_X\text{-a.s.}
\]
Taking expectation and then writing the expectation under the covariate marginal gives
\[
R_P(\pi)=E_P[|\tau_P(X)|{\bf 1}\{\pi(X)\ne\pi_\star(X)\}]
=\int_{\mathcal X}|\tau_P(x)|{\bf 1}\{\pi(x)\ne\pi_\star(x)\}\,dP_X(x).
\]
This is a population identity; no sampling-design premise is used.

statements.statement: Under ass:margin and ass:zero-effect there is C=C(C_m,u_0,alpha) such that for every pi in Pi, P_X(D_pi) <= C R_P(pi)^(alpha/(1+alpha)).
statements.route: Corner R_P(pi)=0: then D_pi has P_X-measure 0 (the disagreement contributes zero |tau|-weighted mass via thm:welfare-identity), so the bound P_X(D_pi)<=C R_P(pi)^{alpha/(1+alpha)} holds trivially (both sides 0 when P_X{tau=0 disagreement}=0 by ass:zero-effect); state this case explicitly. Carry ass:bounded-outcome explicitly because the proof invokes thm:welfare-identity; do not rely on an ambient hidden prerequisite.
statements.justification: Tsybakov margin converts regret into disagreement mass at the fast exponent alpha/(1+alpha); supplies the variance localization for the achievability empirical process.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Fix a deterministic policy pi in Pi and write D=D_pi. By thm:welfare-identity, R_P(pi)=int_D |tau_P(x)| dP_X(x). The integrand is nonnegative and finite by ass:bounded-outcome.

First consider the corner case R_P(pi)=0. Then |tau_P| 1_D=0 P_X-almost surely. Hence P_X(D intersect {0<|tau_P|})=0. On the remaining zero-effect set, ass:zero-effect says either P_X{tau_P=0}=0 or every pi in Pi agrees with pi_star there P_X-almost everywhere. In both alternatives P_X(D intersect {tau_P=0})=0. Therefore P_X(D)=0, so the desired inequality holds with the convention 0^{alpha/(1+alpha)}=0.

Now assume r:=R_P(pi)>0. For any u in (0,u_0], decompose, up to the P_X-null zero-effect disagreement set,
D subset {0<|tau_P|<=u} union (D intersect {|tau_P|>u}).
By ass:margin, P_X(0<|tau_P(X)|<=u)<=C_m u^alpha. By thm:welfare-identity, r>=u P_X(D intersect {|tau_P|>u}), so P_X(D intersect {|tau_P|>u})<=r/u. Thus
P_X(D)<=C_m u^alpha+r/u, 0<u<=u_0.
If r^{1/(1+alpha)}<=u_0, take u=r^{1/(1+alpha)} and obtain P_X(D)<=(C_m+1) r^{alpha/(1+alpha)}. If r^{1/(1+alpha)}>u_0, then P_X(D)<=1<=u_0^{-alpha} r^{alpha/(1+alpha)} when alpha>0, and for alpha=0 simply P_X(D)<=1. Enlarging the constant gives a single C=C(C_m,u_0,alpha) such that P_X(D_pi)<=C R_P(pi)^{alpha/(1+alpha)} for all pi.

statements.statement: For alpha >= 0, gamma > 0, h in (0,1), beta >= 0, at the tight window v=h^beta, u=h^(beta/gamma): u^alpha v^(1/gamma) = h^((alpha+1)beta/gamma), and u^alpha v^(1/gamma) >= h^alpha iff beta <= alpha gamma/(alpha+1), with equality at beta = beta_ag. Hence a block of mass ~ h^alpha, contrast ~ h, propensity ~ h^beta meets the ass:overlap-decay envelope iff beta <= beta_ag, and beta_ag is the least informative admissible weak-arm exponent.
statements.justification: Characterizes which weak-arm propensity layer the overlap-decay envelope admits; pins the maximal (least informative) admissible exponent beta_ag that the converse construction uses.
statements.proof_tex:
Assume \(\gamma>0\). For \(v=h^\beta\) and \(u=h^{\beta/\gamma}\), direct algebra gives
\[
u^\alpha v^{1/\gamma}
=(h^{\beta/\gamma})^\alpha (h^\beta)^{1/\gamma}
=h^{(\alpha+1)\beta/\gamma}.
\]
Since \(0<h<1\), \(h^a\ge h^b\) if and only if \(a\le b\). Hence
\[
u^\alpha v^{1/\gamma}\ge h^\alpha
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\frac{(\alpha+1)\beta}{\gamma}\le \alpha
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\beta\le \frac{\alpha\gamma}{\alpha+1}.
\]
By def:exponents, the right endpoint is \(\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}\), so equality holds at \(\beta=\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}\).

Now consider a block whose \(P_X\)-mass is a constant multiple of \(h^\alpha\), whose contrast height is of order \(h\), and whose weak-arm propensity is of order \(h^\beta\). The overlap-decay condition is most restrictive at the first \(u\)-window for which the constraint \(v\le c_o u^\gamma\) permits \(v\asymp h^\beta\), namely \(u\asymp h^{\beta/\gamma}\). At this window the right side of ass:overlap-decay is, up to constants, \(h^{(\alpha+1)\beta/\gamma}\). The block mass \(h^\alpha\) is therefore admissible up to constants exactly when \(\beta\le\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}\). If \(\beta>\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}\), the allowed envelope is \(o(h^\alpha)\), so no fixed positive multiple of \(h^\alpha\) can satisfy the inequality for all small \(h\). Thus \(\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}\) is the largest admissible weak-arm exponent, equivalently the least informative admissible weak-arm scale.

statements.statement: For all large n the corrected witness laws P_{n,+}, P_{n,-} satisfy the observed-law member-properties of def:law-class, namely ass:bounded-outcome, ass:positivity, ass:margin, ass:zero-effect, ass:overlap-decay, and ass:strict-overlap-endpoint; hence P_{n,+}, P_{n,-} lie in def:law-class P_class(alpha,gamma). The two explicit witness-optimal policies are x -> 1 and x -> 1{x notin B_n}; if these policies belong to Pi, they are available as the two policy actions used by the later two-point lower-bound reduction. (Normalization: the margin window satisfies u_0<2, so an off-block contrast tau_0 in (u_0, 2) exists under bounded outcomes |Y|<=1.)
statements.route: Verify each observed-law member-property of P_class for the explicit construction. Membership in the overlap-decay envelope is the tight-window equality u^alpha v^(1/gamma)=h^alpha at beta=beta_ag (prop:overlap-envelope); margin tail holds because |tau| in {h_n, tau_0} with the h_n level carrying mass <= C_B h_n^alpha on B_n. The global i.i.d. sampling design is kept outside the law class and is invoked later only when the statistical experiment forms product measures from these witness laws.
statements.justification: Replaces the assumed existence of the converse construction (old ass:primitive-cube): the witnesses are CONSTRUCTED and their class membership is DERIVED, not assumed.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Write h=h_n, beta=beta_ag, and m=P_X(B_n)=c_B h^alpha, and let sigma be either + or -. The construction has P_X equal to Lebesgue measure on [0,1], propensity e(x)=q_n on B_n, and propensity e(x)=1/2 on B_n^c. For all large n, h is in (0,1). If beta>0 then q_n=h^beta is in (0,1/2]; if beta=0 the corrected construction uses the fixed value q_n=1/4. Hence 0<e(x)<1 everywhere, proving ass:positivity.

By ass:margin-window, \(u_0<2\), so we may choose an off-block contrast level \(\tau_0\in(u_0,2)\). On B_n the control outcome is deterministically zero, and on the treated active cell the definition fixes a \(\{-1,1\}\)-valued outcome with probabilities \(((1+\sigma h)/2,(1-\sigma h)/2)\), so \(\mu_1(x)=\sigma h\) and \(\mu_0(x)=0\) there. Off the block the common treated/control means are \(\tau_0/2\) and \(-\tau_0/2\), realized by the shared \(\{-1,1\}\)-supported off-block law from def:two-point-witness. This realizes the observed-law specification in def:two-point-witness and proves ass:bounded-outcome. The contrast is tau_{P_{n,+}}(x)=h on B_n, tau_{P_{n,-}}(x)=-h on B_n, and tau_{P_{n,sigma}}(x)=tau_0>u_0 on B_n^c. Thus tau is never zero, so ass:zero-effect holds. The pointwise sign gives the two optimal policies: under P_{n,+}, pi_star(x)=1 for all x; under P_{n,-}, pi_star(x)=0 on B_n and 1 on B_n^c, namely x -> 1{x notin B_n}.

We verify ass:margin. For 0<u<=u_0, the event {0<|tau(X)|<=u} is empty if u<h, and is B_n if h<=u<=u_0, since the off-block contrast is tau_0>u_0. Hence P(0<|tau(X)|<=u) <= c_B h^alpha 1{u>=h} <= c_B u^alpha. Choose the free block constant c_B<=C_m, compatibly with the small-constant information requirement in def:two-point-witness. Then ass:margin holds.

We verify ass:overlap-decay. For 0<u<=u_0 and 0<v<=c_o u^gamma, the event {p_P(X)<=v, 0<|tau(X)|<=u} is empty unless u>=h and v>=q_n; when it is nonempty it is contained in B_n and has mass c_B h^alpha. If gamma=0, the convention is v^(1/gamma)=1, and the desired inequality follows from c_B h^alpha <= C_o u^alpha whenever u>=h after choosing c_B<=C_o. Now suppose gamma>0. If beta>0, the constraints v>=h^beta and v<=c_o u^gamma imply u>=(h^beta/c_o)^(1/gamma), and therefore u^alpha v^(1/gamma) >= (h^beta/c_o)^(alpha/gamma) h^(beta/gamma) = c_o^(-alpha/gamma) h^((alpha+1)beta/gamma). By def:exponents and prop:overlap-envelope, ((alpha+1)beta/gamma)=alpha. Thus the right side of ass:overlap-decay is at least C_o c_o^(-alpha/gamma) h^alpha, and the bound follows after choosing c_B<=C_o c_o^(-alpha/gamma). If gamma>0 and beta=0, then alpha=0; the block mass is c_B, q_n=1/4, and nonemptiness implies v>=1/4, so C_o u^alpha v^(1/gamma)=C_o v^(1/gamma)>=C_o 4^(-1/gamma). Choose c_B<=C_o 4^(-1/gamma). These finitely many smallness requirements can all be imposed once by decreasing c_B.

Finally, ass:strict-overlap-endpoint is active only when gamma=0. In that case q_n=1/4 on B_n and e=1/2 off B_n, so p_P(X)>=1/4 almost surely; the endpoint condition holds with underline_p=1/4 or any smaller fixed constant. Therefore P_{n,+} and P_{n,-} satisfy every observed-law member property listed in def:law-class for all large n, so both laws lie in P_class(alpha,gamma). The two witness-optimal policies were identified above, and if they belong to Pi they are available as the two policy actions used in the two-point lower-bound reduction.

statements.statement: For the def:two-point-witness laws, the per-observation chi-square is chi^2(P_{n,+} || P_{n,-}) <= C m_n q_n h_n^2 with m_n=P_X(B_n)~h_n^alpha and q_n~h_n^{beta_ag}, hence ~ h_n^{2+alpha+beta_ag}; for h_n=n^{-1/(2+alpha+beta_ag)} the n-fold divergence is bounded, chi^2(P_{n,+}^{⊗n} || P_{n,-}^{⊗n}) <= C (equivalently 1+chi^2(P^{⊗n}) = (1+O(h_n^{2+alpha+beta_ag}))^n <= e^{C}).
statements.route: The two laws differ only on the treated active cell {X in B_n, A=1} of mass m_n q_n, where def:two-point-witness makes the outcome explicitly \{-1,1\}-valued with probabilities (1 \pm h_n)/2 for Y=1. The induced two-point experiment has chi-square ~ h_n^2. Restrict to the charged cell and tensorize. Balance n h_n^(2+alpha+beta_ag) ~ 1.
statements.justification: The information-cost half of the Le Cam two-point bound, derived from the explicit construction.
statements.proof_tex:
Let \(m_n=P_X(B_n)=c_Bh_n^\alpha\). The two laws differ only on the cell \(\{X\in B_n,A=1\}\), whose probability under either law is \(m_nq_n\). Conditional on that cell, the outcome is supported on \(\{-1,1\}\) with probabilities \((1+h_n)/2,(1-h_n)/2\) under \(P_{n,+}\) and the probabilities reversed under \(P_{n,-}\). For \(h_n\le1/2\), the Bernoulli chi-square divergence is
\[
\chi^2\!\left(\frac{1+h_n}{2},\frac{1-h_n}{2}\right)
=\frac{h_n^2}{(1-h_n)/2}+\frac{h_n^2}{(1+h_n)/2}
=\frac{4h_n^2}{1-h_n^2}
\le 8h_n^2.
\]
Since the mixing probabilities and all other conditional laws are identical, the per-observation divergence is the cell mass times this conditional divergence:
\[
\chi^2(P_{n,+}\|P_{n,-})\le 8m_nq_nh_n^2
\le C h_n^{2+\alpha+\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}},
\]
with the usual interpretation \(q_n\asymp h_n^{\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}}\), including the constant weak-arm scale when \(\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}=0\). By def:exponents, \(D_{\alpha,\gamma}=2+\alpha+\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}\) and \(h_n=n^{-1/D_{\alpha,\gamma}}\), so the right side is at most \(C/n\).

Chi-square tensorizes as
\[
1+\chi^2(P_{n,+}^{\otimes n}\|P_{n,-}^{\otimes n})
=(1+\chi^2(P_{n,+}\|P_{n,-}))^n.
\]
Therefore
\[
1+\chi^2(P_{n,+}^{\otimes n}\|P_{n,-}^{\otimes n})
\le (1+C/n)^n\le e^C,
\]
and the \(n\)-fold chi-square divergence is bounded by a constant independent of \(n\).

statements.statement: For the def:two-point-witness laws, inf_{pi in Pi} max_{sigma in {+,-}} R_{P_{n,sigma}}(pi) >= c h_n^(1+alpha): the optimal policies are opposite on B_n, so any pi misclassifies B_n under at least one law and pays regret >= (1/2) P_X(B_n) h_n ~ h_n^(1+alpha).
statements.route: By thm:welfare-identity, regret on B_n equals |tau| times disagreement mass. Since tau_{P_{n,+}}=+h_n and tau_{P_{n,-}}=-h_n on B_n, pi_star flips sign across the two laws on B_n; any fixed pi disagrees with one of them on all of B_n, giving regret >= h_n P_X(B_n)/... ~ h_n^(1+alpha). Carry ass:bounded-outcome explicitly because the proof invokes thm:welfare-identity; do not rely on an ambient hidden prerequisite.
statements.justification: The separation half of the Le Cam two-point bound, derived from the construction.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Write h=h_n and m=P_X(B_n). By def:two-point-witness, on B_n the contrasts are tau_{P_{n,+}}=h and tau_{P_{n,-}}=-h. Therefore the two optimal labels on B_n are opposite: pi_star,+ equals 1 and pi_star,- equals 0 on B_n. For any deterministic binary policy pi and every x in B_n, exactly one of the two indicators 1{pi(x) != pi_star,+(x)} and 1{pi(x) != pi_star,-(x)} is equal to one.

Applying thm:welfare-identity under each witness law, and using ass:bounded-outcome to justify that identity, gives
R_{P_{n,+}}(pi)+R_{P_{n,-}}(pi) >= h int_{B_n} [1{pi(x) != pi_star,+(x)}+1{pi(x) != pi_star,-(x)}] dP_X(x) = h P_X(B_n)=h m.
Hence max_{sigma in {+,-}} R_{P_{n,sigma}}(pi) >= h m/2. The construction has m=c_B h^alpha, so max_sigma R_{P_{n,sigma}}(pi) >= (c_B/2) h^{1+alpha}. Taking the infimum over pi in Pi proves the claim with c=c_B/2.

statements.statement: In-core two-point testing lemma. Assume the squared density deviation (dP_+/dP_- - 1)^2 is P_- -integrable (well-posedness, so chi^2 is the genuine divergence and not a junk Bochner value). If chi^2(P_+^{\otimes n} || P_-^{\otimes n}) <= C_chi, then every test psi has P_+^n(psi != +)+P_-^n(psi != -) >= c(C_chi)>0; in particular the conclusion follows from a per-observation chi-square bound <= c_0/n using the product chi-square identity. Prove this from total-variation testing risk, Cauchy-Schwarz/Pinsker-type TV <= sqrt(chi^2), and (1+x/n)^n <= exp(x), not by citation alone.
statements.route: Derive: minimal testing-error sum >= 1-TV(P_+^n,P_-^n); TV <= sqrt(chi^2/2) or another explicit chi-square-to-TV bound; product chi-square <= exp(c_0)-1. Choose constants so the witness chi-square constant is small enough, yielding a positive c_1. Include the elementary product-chi-square/KL comparison as part of the proof or as explicit dependency nodes with proof_tex.
statements.proof_tex:
Let \(P=P_+^{\otimes n}\), \(Q=P_-^{\otimes n}\), and let \(\psi\) be any test taking values in \(\{+,-\}\). The sum of testing errors is
\[
  P(\psi\ne +)+Q(\psi\ne -)=P(\psi=-)+Q(\psi=+).
\]
The minimal value over all tests equals \(1-\|P-Q\|_{TV}\): this follows by writing the risk of the rejection region \(A=\{\psi=+\}\) as \(P(A^c)+Q(A)=1-P(A)+Q(A)\), and maximizing \(P(A)-Q(A)\) over measurable \(A\), whose supremum is total variation.

We now bound total variation by chi-square in a form that is positive for every finite chi-square bound. Assume \(P\ll Q\), write \(L=dP/dQ\), and set \(A=\{L\ge1\}\). Then \(t:=\|P-Q\|_{TV}=P(A)-Q(A)=\int_A(L-1)dQ\). By Cauchy-Schwarz,
\[
  t^2\le Q(A)\int_A(L-1)^2dQ\le Q(A)\chi^2(P\|Q).
\]
Since \(P(A)=Q(A)+t\le1\), we have \(Q(A)\le1-t\). If \(\chi^2(P\|Q)\le C_\chi\), then
\[
  t^2\le C_\chi(1-t).
\]
Solving the quadratic inequality gives
\[
  t\le \rho(C_\chi):={\sqrt{C_\chi^2+4C_\chi}-C_\chi\over2}<1.
\]
Therefore every test satisfies
\[
  P_+^n(\psi\ne +)+P_-^n(\psi\ne -)
  \ge 1-\rho(C_\chi)=:c(C_\chi)>0.
\]

For the per-observation implication, if \(\chi^2(P_+\|P_-)\le c_0/n\), then the product identity gives
\[
  1+
  \chi^2(P_+^{\otimes n}\|P_-^{\otimes n})
  =\{1+
  \chi^2(P_+\|P_-)\}^n
  \le (1+c_0/n)^n\le e^{c_0}.
\]
Thus \(\chi^2(P_+^{\otimes n}\|P_-^{\otimes n})\le e^{c_0}-1\), and the preceding bound applies with \(C_\chi=e^{c_0}-1\).

statements.statement: M_n(alpha,gamma) = inf_{pi_hat} sup_{P in P_class} E_P R_P(pi_hat) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} for all large n, with c > 0 independent of n.
statements.route: Le Cam two-point reduction: the two in-class witnesses (lem:witness-membership) are statistically near-indistinguishable (bounded n-fold divergence, lem:two-point-divergence) yet force opposite labels with regret separation ~ h_n^(1+alpha) for any realized Pi-valued decision rule (lem:regret-separation). No witness-optimal-policy membership premise is needed; two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le converts the bounded divergence into a constant testing-error floor, yielding M_n >= c h_n^(1+alpha) = c n^{-r_star}.
statements.justification: The novel sharp converse: the margin/one-sided-overlap minimax regret floor, with the least-favorable family CONSTRUCTED from primitives (no assumed cube).
statements.gap: AtheyWager2021 give the n^{-1/2} efficient rate under strict overlap; KitagawaTetenov2018 the VC achievability; Audibert-Tsybakov2007 the margin converse without overlap decay. None give the coupled margin x one-sided-overlap-decay regret floor r_*(alpha,gamma).
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Fix n large enough that lem:witness-membership and lem:two-point-divergence apply, and write P_+=P_{n,+} and P_-=P_{n,-}. By lem:witness-membership, both alternatives belong to P_class(alpha,gamma). Under the global sampling design ass:iid, the corresponding n-sample experiments are the product measures P_+^{\otimes n} and P_-^{\otimes n}. Therefore, for any measurable Pi-valued estimator pi_hat, the risk sup_{P in P_class} E_P R_P(pi_hat) is at least max{E_{P_+} R_{P_+}(pi_hat), E_{P_-} R_{P_-}(pi_hat)}.

Let Delta_n=c_sep h_n^{1+alpha} be the separation from lem:regret-separation. For each realized data set, define a test psi by psi=+ if R_{P_+}(pi_hat)<=R_{P_-}(pi_hat), and psi=- otherwise, with arbitrary tie-breaking on equality. On the event {psi=-}, if R_{P_+}(pi_hat)<Delta_n/2, then the definition of psi gives R_{P_-}(pi_hat)<Delta_n/2 as well, contradicting lem:regret-separation. Hence R_{P_+}(pi_hat)>=Delta_n/2 on {psi=-}. Similarly, R_{P_-}(pi_hat)>=Delta_n/2 on {psi=+}. Consequently
E_{P_+} R_{P_+}(pi_hat)+E_{P_-} R_{P_-}(pi_hat) >= (Delta_n/2) {P_+^n(psi=-)+P_-^n(psi=+)}.

By lem:two-point-divergence, chi^2(P_+^{\otimes n} || P_-^{\otimes n}) is bounded by a constant C_chi independent of n. Lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq gives c_test=c(C_chi)>0 such that every test satisfies P_+^n(psi=-)+P_-^n(psi=+)>=c_test. Hence max_sigma E_{P_sigma} R_{P_sigma}(pi_hat) >= c_test Delta_n/4. Since pi_hat was arbitrary, taking the infimum over all measurable Pi-valued estimators in def:minimax-regret yields M_n(alpha,gamma)>=c h_n^{1+alpha}.

Finally, def:exponents gives h_n=n^{-1/D_ag} and r_star(alpha,gamma)=(1+alpha)/D_ag. Thus h_n^{1+alpha}=n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}, proving M_n(alpha,gamma)>=c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} for all large n.

statements.statement: CONDITIONAL ACHIEVABILITY / CONSTRUCT-AND-DETERMINE. For the regime-indexed conditional upper risk U_n from def:upper-risk, with one fixed nuisance regime (a,c,C_mu,C_prod) and the deterministic schedule selected by def:feasible-rate for that regime, derive the honest exponent r_up for the plain cross-fit clipped-AIPW 1/n-ERM using only the crude clipped-score envelope from weights bounded by 1/q (second-moment scale q^{-2}) plus deterministic lem:clip-bias controlled by ass:overlap-decay and ass:margin/localization. This is a conditional achievability bound over def:law-class on the exact side-condition domain bundled into def:upper-risk, not a smaller law class and not a claim that nuisance rates, fixed-K cross-fitting, offset control, or optimal-in-class are implied by def:law-class.
statements.route: Use lem:feasible-erm-basic-inequality, lem:crossfit-localized-process-reduction, and lem:localized-vc-self-bound to construct one pooled self-localized inequality for the 1/n ERM; use only the crude q^{-2} clipped-score envelope and lem:localized-clipped-drift-bound/lem:clip-bias for drift. Do not introduce a new law-class split, marginal-tail shortcut, clipped-propensity-variance-stability, known propensity, oracle denominator, or an unconditional minimax upper claim.
statements.justification: Construct-target: codex DERIVES the feasible weak-overlap exponent from the variance/clip-bias tradeoff (not asserted) -> proof matches statement; upper half of the bracket.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. The target functional is the welfare regret \(R_P(\pi)=V_P(\pi_\star)-V_P(\pi)\) fixed in def:welfare-regret; the rate statement itself is over the observed-law class def:law-class. This result concerns the estimation behavior of the explicit cross-fit clipped-AIPW \(1/n\)-ERM in def:feasible-erm.

Fix one nuisance regime \((a,c,C_\mu,C_{prod})\). The upper-risk functional in def:upper-risk takes the supremum over laws in def:law-class together with the explicitly stated estimator-side and policy-side conditions: ass:optimal-in-class, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents, ass:policy-class, ass:vc-localized-envelope, ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope, and ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count. These conditions are not asserted to follow from def:law-class.

First let \(\gamma>0\). Def:feasible-rate chooses \(q_n=q_0n^{-s_{feas}}\) and \(u_n=\bar u n^{-t_{feas}}\) on the compact feasible set \(0\le s_{feas}\le1/2\), \(0\le t_{feas}\le s_{feas}/\gamma\). Hence \(q_n\le c_ou_n^\gamma\) for all large \(n\), and lem:crude-localized-master-bound applies on exactly that conditional domain:
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le C\left\{n^{-r_\star}+(nq_n^2)^{-A_\alpha}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}{q_n}+r_{\mu,n}u_n^{\alpha/2}q_n^{1/(2\gamma)}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}^2}{u_n}\right\}(\log n)^p.
\]
By lem:clip-balance-exponent and ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents, the four feasible stochastic/nuisance terms are bounded by \(Cn^{-g_{joint}(\alpha,\gamma,a,c)}\). Therefore
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le Cn^{-\min\{r_\star,g_{joint}\}}(\log n)^p
=Cn^{-r_{up}}(\log n)^p,
\]
where \(r_{up}=r_{feas}\) is the exponent constructed in def:feasible-rate.

For \(\gamma=0\), def:feasible-rate uses fixed \(q_n=q_0\le\underline p/2\). Lem:crude-localized-master-bound gives
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le C\{n^{-A_\alpha}+r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}\}(\log n)^p.
\]
Since ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents gives \(r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}\le Cn^{-c}\),
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le Cn^{-\min\{A_\alpha,c\}}(\log n)^p=Cn^{-r_{up}}(\log n)^p.
\]
Taking the supremum over the already-declared domain in def:upper-risk proves
\[
U_n(\alpha,\gamma,a,c;\widehat\eta)\le Cn^{-r_{up}}(\log n)^p.
\]
This derives the closed honest exponent for the plain feasible clipped-AIPW ERM from the crude \(q^{-2}\) score envelope and the deterministic drift controls; no new law-class split or rate-buying propensity stability assumption is introduced.

statements.statement: Lower-bound headline only. M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} for all large n, where r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}).
statements.route: Immediate corollary of thm:minimax-lower and def:exponents. Do not add witness-policy membership, feasible-upper, or open-residual dependencies to this theorem.
statements.justification: Headline lower bound: the explicit weak-overlap policy-regret converse exponent over the baseline observed-law class, without packaging any conditional feasible upper or open tightness residual into this theorem.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. The theorem is the lower-bound-only headline. By thm:minimax-lower, there exist constants c>0 and N<infinity such that for all n>=N, M_n(alpha,gamma)>=c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}. By def:exponents, D_ag=2+alpha+beta_ag and r_star(alpha,gamma)=(1+alpha)/D_ag=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}). This is exactly the displayed sharp lower-bound characterization. No feasible-upper theorem and no tightness residual is used in this corollary.

statements.statement: OPEN: In the strict-gap branch of the conditional feasible achievability bound for the regime-indexed risk U_n over def:law-class plus explicit side conditions, determine whether any genuinely feasible estimator under estimated cross-fitted nuisances can attain the converse exponent r_star(alpha,gamma), or whether weak-arm nuisance learning imposes the slower conditional exponent derived in oeq:feasible-upper.
statements.route: Compare the baseline converse benchmark from thm:minimax-lower with the conditional U_n upper bound from oeq:feasible-upper. Keep the scope distinction explicit; do not introduce a new law-class split node and do not feed this residual back into thm:rate-characterization.
statements.justification: Residual tightness question for the weak-overlap bracket (open).
statements.proof_tex: If g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)>=r_star(alpha,gamma), then oeq:feasible-upper and thm:minimax-lower match up to logarithmic factors for the explicit feasible ERM: c n^{-r_star} <= M_n and U_n <= C n^{-r_star}(log n)^p. If g_joint<r_star, the current results give the honest bracket c n^{-r_star} for the baseline minimax lower benchmark and U_n <= C n^{-g_joint}(log n)^p for the specified feasible clipped-AIPW ERM, with tightness unresolved.

statements.statement:
Exact clipped-score drift identity. For any measurable plug-in nuisance triple \(\bar\eta=(\bar\mu_0,\bar\mu_1,\bar e)\), with \(\bar e_q=\min\{1-q,\max\{q,\bar e\}\}\) and \(\Delta_a=\bar\mu_a-\mu_a\), the conditional mean drift satisfies
\[
E_P[\Gamma_q(O;\bar\eta)\mid X=x]-\tau_P(x)
=(\bar e_q(x)-e_P(x))\left(\frac{\Delta_1(x)}{\bar e_q(x)}+\frac{\Delta_0(x)}{1-\bar e_q(x)}\right).
\]
Hence cancellation holds only where \(\bar e_q(x)=e_P(x)\) or both regression errors vanish; it does not follow merely from \(p_P(x)>q\). Under the frozen assumptions alone, no additive clipped-region bound of the form asserted in the current lemma is derivable.
statements.route: Pure algebra from def:clipped-aipw-score: take E[.|X=a-arm] using E[Y|A=a,X]=mu_a; the IPW weights e/ebar_q and (1-e)/(1-ebar_q) give the displayed identity (DR cancellation needs ebar_q=e_P, NOT p>q). Then Cauchy-Schwarz for the policy-weighted bound. This is an EQUALITY + one inequality; prove it directly, do not assert cancellation.
statements.justification: Estimated-nuisance clip-bias bound — the piece codex could not derive from a high-level route; explicit sub-lemma for oeq:feasible-upper.
statements.proof_tex:
Fix \(x\) and write \(e=e_P(x)\), \(\bar e_q=\bar e_q(x)\), \(\mu_a=\mu_a(x)\), \(\bar\mu_a=\bar\mu_a(x)\), and \(\Delta_a=\bar\mu_a-\mu_a\). By def:clipped-aipw-score and the defining conditional means,
\[
E_P[\Gamma_q(O;\bar\eta)\mid X=x]
=\bar\mu_1-\bar\mu_0+\frac{e}{\bar e_q}(\mu_1-\bar\mu_1)-\frac{1-e}{1-\bar e_q}(\mu_0-\bar\mu_0).
\]
Substitute \(\bar\mu_a=\mu_a+\Delta_a\):
\[
E_P[\Gamma_q(O;\bar\eta)\mid X=x]
=\mu_1-\mu_0+\Delta_1-\Delta_0-\frac{e}{\bar e_q}\Delta_1+\frac{1-e}{1-\bar e_q}\Delta_0.
\]
Since \(\tau_P(x)=\mu_1-\mu_0\), the drift equals
\[
\Delta_1\left(1-\frac{e}{\bar e_q}\right)
+\Delta_0\left(-1+\frac{1-e}{1-\bar e_q}\right)
=(\bar e_q-e)\left(\frac{\Delta_1}{\bar e_q}+\frac{\Delta_0}{1-\bar e_q}\right),
\]
which is the displayed identity. Positivity and clipping ensure the denominators are nonzero. The identity shows that double-robust cancellation occurs when \(\bar e_q=e_P\) or \(\Delta_0=\Delta_1=0\); the event \(p_P>q\) alone does not imply \(\bar e_q=e_P\) for an estimated propensity, so no additional clipped-region cancellation follows from the frozen assumptions.

statements.statement: Under ass:policy-class, def:feasible-erm defines a measurable Pi-valued estimator satisfying Vhat_{n,q}(pi_hat_n) >= sup_{pi in Pi} Vhat_{n,q}(pi) - 1/n. Hence for every comparator pi^b in Pi, P_n[(pi_hat_n-pi^b) Gamma_q] >= -1/n; under ass:optimal-in-class this applies to pi^b=pi_star.
statements.route: Prove by reducing the empirical criterion to the countable pointwise-dense skeleton Pi_0, choosing the first 1/n-near-maximizing index, and using countability for measurability. This constructs the estimator; do not assume an exact argmax exists.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Let \(\Pi_0=(\pi_j)_{j\ge1}\) be the countable pointwise-dense subclass supplied by ass:policy-class. For a realized sample and fixed cross-fitted nuisance scores \(\Gamma_i=\Gamma_q(O_i;\widehat\eta^{(-k(i))})\), the empirical criterion is
\[
\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi)=n^{-1}\sum_{i=1}^n \pi(X_i)\Gamma_i .
\]
If \(\pi\in\Pi\), pointwise density gives \(\pi_{j_\ell}(x)\to\pi(x)\) for every \(x\). Since \(\widehat V_{n,q}\) is a finite sum over the observed covariates, \(\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi_{j_\ell})\to\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi)\). Hence
\[
\sup_{\pi\in\Pi}\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi)=\sup_{j\ge1}\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi_j).
\]
The right side is a countable supremum of measurable sample functions. The index \(j_n\) in def:feasible-erm is the first integer satisfying \(\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi_j)\ge \sup_k \widehat V_{n,q}(\pi_k)-1/n\); this event is measurable because it is determined by countably many measurable inequalities. Therefore \(\widehat\pi_n=\pi_{j_n}\) is a measurable \(\Pi\)-valued estimator and, by construction,
\[
\widehat V_{n,q}(\widehat\pi_n)\ge \sup_{\pi\in\Pi}\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi)-1/n.
\]
For any comparator \(\pi^b\in\Pi\), subtracting \(\widehat V_{n,q}(\pi^b)\) gives
\[
P_n[(\widehat\pi_n-\pi^b)\Gamma_q]\ge -1/n.
\]
Under ass:optimal-in-class, \(\pi_\star\in\Pi\), so the comparator inequality applies to \(\pi^b=\pi_\star\).

statements.statement: Let rho_n=(B^2/n)^{A_alpha}(log n)^p with B>=1. For a centered Pi-indexed process z_pi whose offset positive part is controlled by the proved DAG node lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control, any Pi-indexed estimator pi_tilde satisfying R_P(pi_tilde) <= 2|z_{pi_tilde}| + delta_n obeys E_P R_P(pi_tilde) <= C{rho_n+delta_n}; if delta_n<=1/n, the delta_n term is absorbed into rho_n.
statements.route: Pure deterministic/expectation algebra using the offset bound already proved by lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control. Dependency direction is crossfit-localized-offset-control -> localized-vc-self-bound -> crude-localized-master-bound.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Set
\[
S=\sup_{\pi\in\Pi}\{2|z_\pi|-R_P(\pi)/4\}_+ .
\]
For the selected policy \(\widetilde\pi\), the assumed inequality gives
\[
R_P(\widetilde\pi)\le 2|z_{\widetilde\pi}|+\delta_n .
\]
Subtracting \(R_P(\widetilde\pi)/4\) from both sides gives
\[
\frac34R_P(\widetilde\pi)
\le 2|z_{\widetilde\pi}|-\frac14R_P(\widetilde\pi)+\delta_n
\le S+\delta_n .
\]
Therefore
\[
R_P(\widetilde\pi)\le \frac43S+\frac43\delta_n .
\]
Taking expectations and using the proved offset-control node lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control gives
\[
E_PR_P(\widetilde\pi)
\le C(B^2/n)^{A_\alpha}(\log n)^p+C\delta_n
=C\{\rho_n+\delta_n\}.
\]
Finally, \(A_\alpha=(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha)<1\) for finite \(\alpha\ge0\) and \(B\ge1\), so for all large \(n\), \(n^{-1}\le C(B^2/n)^{A_\alpha}(\log n)^p\) after increasing the constant. Thus any \(\delta_n\le1/n\) is absorbed into the \(C\rho_n\) term.

statements.statement: Under def:law-class and the estimator-side/policy conditions ass:optimal-in-class, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents, ass:policy-class, ass:vc-localized-envelope, ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope, and ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count, the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW 1/n-ERM of def:feasible-erm satisfies E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C{n^{-r_star}+(n q^2)^{-A_alpha}+r_mu_n r_e_n/q+r_mu_n u^{alpha/2}q^{1/(2 gamma)}+r_mu_n^2/u}(log n)^p for gamma>0 whenever q<=c_o u^gamma. For gamma=0 with fixed q<=underline_p/2 it satisfies E_P R_P(pi_hat_n)<=C{n^{-A_alpha}+r_mu_n r_e_n}(log n)^p.
statements.route: Use lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control first, then lem:localized-vc-self-bound. Do not add dependencies from offset-control back to self-bound, master-bound, def:upper-risk, or oeq:feasible-upper.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Fix \(P\) in def:law-class and fix the cross-fitted nuisance estimates, policy class, fold scheme, and empirical-process side conditions appearing in the statement. Let \(\widehat\pi=\widehat\pi_n\). The proof is conditional on the training folds; all bounds are uniform in the folds and are then integrated over the training randomness.

For \(i\in I_k\), write
\[
\Gamma_i=\Gamma_q(O_i;\widehat\eta^{(-k)}),\qquad
m_k(x)=E_P[\Gamma_q(O;\widehat\eta^{(-k)})\mid X=x]=\tau_P(x)+b_k(x).
\]
The identity defining \(b_k\) is exactly lem:clip-bias applied with the foldwise nuisance triple. By lem:feasible-erm-basic-inequality, with comparator \(\pi_\star\in\Pi\) from ass:optimal-in-class,
\[
P_n[(\widehat\pi-\pi_\star)\Gamma_i]\ge -1/n .
\]
Define the pooled centered process
\[
G_{cf}(\pi)=P_n[(\pi-\pi_\star)\Gamma_i]-\sum_{k=1}^Kw_kP[(\pi-\pi_\star)m_k],\qquad w_k=|I_k|/n.
\]
Using def:welfare-regret and thm:welfare-identity,
\[
P[(\pi-\pi_\star)\tau_P]=-R_P(\pi),
\]
so the basic inequality gives, with \(r=R_P(\widehat\pi)\),
\[
r\le |G_{cf}(\widehat\pi)|+\left|\sum_{k=1}^Kw_kP[(\widehat\pi-\pi_\star)b_k]\right|+1/n .
\]
The stochastic process is controlled only through the crude clipped-score envelope. Lem:crude-clipped-score-envelope gives \(|\Gamma_i|\le C/q\), and since \(\pi-\pi_\star\) vanishes outside \(D_\pi\), the centered increments have envelope \(B=C/q\) and conditional second moment bounded by \(CB^2P_X(D_\pi)\). By ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count and ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope, lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control applies to \(G_{cf}\), and then lem:localized-vc-self-bound controls the random-radius choice \(\widehat\pi\) once the deterministic drift is absorbed.

Assume \(\gamma>0\) and \(q\le c_ou^\gamma\). Lem:localized-clipped-drift-bound, applied foldwise and averaged over \(k\), gives
\[
\left|\sum_{k=1}^Kw_kP[(\widehat\pi-\pi_\star)b_k]\right|
\le C\left\{\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}q+r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}+r_{\mu,n}(r/u)^{1/2}\right\}.
\]
By Young's inequality,
\[
Cr_{\mu,n}(r/u)^{1/2}\le r/4+Cr_{\mu,n}^2/u .
\]
Absorbing \(r/4\) into the left side yields
\[
r\le 2|G_{cf}(\widehat\pi)|+C\left\{\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}q+r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}^2}{u}+\frac1n\right\}.
\]
Now apply lem:localized-vc-self-bound with \(B=C/q\). Since \(q\le1/2\) and \(A_\alpha\le1\), the \(1/n\) term is absorbed by the stochastic term for all large \(n\). This gives
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le C\left\{(nq^2)^{-A_\alpha}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}q+r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}^2}{u}\right\}(\log n)^p .
\]
The bounded oracle policy-learning centered process with score \((\pi-\pi_\star)\tau_P\) has envelope bounded by a universal constant and second moment bounded by \(CP_X(D_\pi)\); the same localized VC argument gives an additional valid oracle/localization contribution \(n^{-A_\alpha}(\log n)^p\). Because \(\beta_{\alpha,\gamma}\ge0\), def:exponents gives \(r_\star=(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha+\beta_{\alpha,\gamma})\le A_\alpha\), so \(n^{-A_\alpha}\le n^{-r_\star}\). Retaining this benchmark term explicitly gives
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le C\left\{n^{-r_\star}+(nq^2)^{-A_\alpha}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}q+r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}^2}{u}\right\}(\log n)^p .
\]

For \(\gamma=0\), ass:strict-overlap-endpoint and fixed \(q\le\underline p/2\) imply, by lem:localized-clipped-drift-bound,
\[
\left|\sum_{k=1}^Kw_kP[(\widehat\pi-\pi_\star)b_k]\right|\le Cr_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}.
\]
The clipped-score envelope is then constant, so the same offset/self-bound argument gives
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le C\{n^{-A_\alpha}+r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}\}(\log n)^p .
\]
The proof uses the single data-driven ERM in def:feasible-erm and the crude \(q^{-2}\) second-moment scale; it does not use a marginal-tail shortcut, known propensity, oracle denominator, new law class, or clipped-propensity variance-stability premise.

statements.statement: Let kappa=alpha/(2+2 alpha). For an i.i.d. sample of size m, let Z_m(r)=sup_{pi in Pi:R_P(pi)<=r} |(P_m-P) g_pi|, where the centered increments g_pi have envelope B and conditional second moment bounded by C B^2 P_X(D_pi). Under ass:vc-localized-envelope and thm:margin-localization, E_P Z_m(r) <= C B m^(-1/2) r^kappa (log m)^p for every regret radius r.
statements.route: Combine thm:margin-localization, which turns regret radius r into disagreement mass r^{alpha/(1+alpha)}, with the localized VC envelope from ass:vc-localized-envelope. This is the fixed-radius stochastic-process input used later by both the pooled cross-fit reduction and the generic self-bounding lemma.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Fix a regret radius \(r\ge0\) and set \(\Pi(r)=\{\pi\in\Pi:R_P(\pi)\le r\}\). For every \(\pi\in\Pi(r)\), thm:margin-localization gives
\[
P_X(D_\pi)\le C R_P(\pi)^{\alpha/(1+\alpha)}\le C r^{\alpha/(1+\alpha)} .
\]
The increments \(g_\pi\) are centered, have envelope \(B\), and satisfy the conditional second-moment bound \(E[g_\pi^2]\le C B^2P_X(D_\pi)\) by the hypothesis of the lemma. The atomic fixed-radius empirical-process assumption ass:vc-localized-envelope is exactly the finite-VC localized bound for such pointwise measurable policy-indexed classes after applying the preceding margin localization. With sample size \(m\), it yields
\[
E_P\sup_{\pi\in\Pi(r)} |(P_m-P)g_\pi|
\le C B m^{-1/2} r^{\alpha/(2+2\alpha)}(\log m)^p .
\]
Since \(\kappa=\alpha/(2+2\alpha)\), this is the claimed bound for \(Z_m(r)\). The cases \(r=0\) and \(\alpha=0\) are included by the conventions in thm:margin-localization and by enlarging the universal constant.

statements.statement: Let Z_cf(r) be the pooled cross-fit localized process obtained by averaging centered evaluation-fold increments. If ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count holds, then conditional on the training folds each balanced evaluation fold is i.i.d.; if the centered foldwise increments have envelope B and conditional second moment bounded by C B^2 P_X(D_pi), then under ass:vc-localized-envelope and thm:margin-localization, E_P[Z_cf(r) | training folds] <= C B n^(-1/2) r^(alpha/(2+2 alpha)) (log n)^p, hence the same bound unconditionally.
statements.route: Decompose the pooled process as the weighted average of the K evaluation-fold processes. Conditional on the training folds, each fold is an i.i.d. sample with fixed nuisances, so lem:localized-vc-process-bound applies foldwise. Balanced folds let the weighted sum of n_k^{-1/2} terms collapse back to n^{-1/2}.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Let the deterministic folds be \(I_1,\ldots,I_K\), write \(n_k=|I_k|\), and set \(w_k=n_k/n\). For the foldwise fixed nuisance object define
\[
Z_k(r)=\sup_{\pi:R_P(\pi)\le r}\left|(P_{I_k}-P)g_{k,\pi}\right|,
\]
where \(g_{k,\pi}\) denotes the centered evaluation-fold increment. The pooled process has the form
\[
Z_{cf}(r)=\sup_{\pi:R_P(\pi)\le r}\left|\sum_{k=1}^K w_k(P_{I_k}-P)g_{k,\pi}\right| .
\]
By the triangle inequality,
\[
Z_{cf}(r)\le \sum_{k=1}^K w_k Z_k(r).
\]
For a fixed fold \(k\), condition on the training sigma-field used to build \(g_{k,\pi}\). By ass:iid and the deterministic fold partition in def:feasible-erm, the observations in \(I_k\) are then i.i.d. from \(P\), and the foldwise class is fixed. The assumed envelope and second-moment bounds allow lem:localized-vc-process-bound to be applied conditionally, giving
\[
E_P[Z_k(r)\mid \mathcal T_k]
\le C B n_k^{-1/2} r^{\alpha/(2+2\alpha)}(\log n_k)^p .
\]
Taking expectations and summing with weights gives
\[
E_P Z_{cf}(r)
\le C B r^{\alpha/(2+2\alpha)}\sum_{k=1}^K w_kn_k^{-1/2}(\log n_k)^p .
\]
By ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count, \(K\) is fixed and the folds are balanced, so \(n_k\asymp n\), \(\log n_k\le C\log n\), and \(\sum_kw_kn_k^{-1/2}\le C n^{-1/2}\). Therefore
\[
E_P Z_{cf}(r)
\le C B n^{-1/2} r^{\alpha/(2+2\alpha)}(\log n)^p .
\]
The displayed conditional estimate is the foldwise conditional estimate used in cross-fitting; integrating over the training folds gives the unconditional bound claimed for the pooled process.

statements.statement: For the pooled cross-fit centered process G_cf(pi) from lem:crude-localized-master-bound, if the foldwise centered increments have envelope B and conditional second moment bounded by C B^2 P_X(D_pi), then under ass:iid, ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count, ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope, and thm:margin-localization, E_P sup_{pi in Pi}{2|G_cf(pi)|-R_P(pi)/4}_+ <= C (B^2/n)^{A_alpha}(log n)^p.
statements.route: Prove this from the atomic i.i.d. offset empirical-process assumption ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope by conditioning on the training folds, applying the assumption to each evaluation fold as an i.i.d. sample of size n_k, and then combining across fixed balanced folds. This lemma is an input to lem:localized-vc-self-bound; it must never depend on lem:localized-vc-self-bound or oeq:feasible-upper.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Write the pooled centered cross-fit process as
\[
G_{cf}(\pi)=\sum_{k=1}^K w_k H_k(\pi),\qquad w_k=|I_k|/n,
\]
where \(H_k(\pi)\) is the centered evaluation-fold empirical average with the foldwise nuisance estimates held fixed and \(n_k=|I_k|\). Conditional on the training folds, the increments in each \(H_k\) have envelope \(B\) and conditional second moment bounded by \(C B^2P_X(D_\pi)\), by the hypothesis of the lemma. By ass:iid and the deterministic fold partition in def:feasible-erm, the observations in the evaluation fold \(I_k\) remain i.i.d. from \(P\) after conditioning on the training sigma-field, while the foldwise nuisance object is fixed. Therefore the atomic i.i.d. offset assumption ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope applies to \(H_k\) with sample size \(n_k\), giving
\[
E\left[\sup_{\pi\in\Pi}\{2|H_k(\pi)|-R_P(\pi)/4\}_+\mid \mathcal T_k\right]
\le C(B^2/n_k)^{A_\alpha}(\log n_k)^p.
\]
The regret localization required by that assumption is supplied by thm:margin-localization, whose bounded-outcome prerequisite is part of the displayed dependency list.

For the pooled process, use \(|\sum_k w_kH_k(\pi)|\le\sum_kw_k|H_k(\pi)|\) and convexity of \(x\mapsto x_+\):
\[
\{2|G_{cf}(\pi)|-R_P(\pi)/4\}_+
\le \sum_{k=1}^K w_k\{2|H_k(\pi)|-R_P(\pi)/4\}_+.
\]
Taking the supremum over \(\pi\), expectation, and then summing the foldwise bounds yields
\[
E_P\sup_{\pi\in\Pi}\{2|G_{cf}(\pi)|-R_P(\pi)/4\}_+
\le C\sum_{k=1}^K w_k(B^2/n_k)^{A_\alpha}(\log n_k)^p.
\]
By ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count, \(K\) is fixed and the deterministic folds are balanced, so \(n_k\asymp n\) and \(\log n_k\le C\log n\). Hence the last display is at most
\[
C(B^2/n)^{A_\alpha}(\log n)^p.
\]
This proves the claimed offset control. No propensity-stability, oracle-denominator, or marginal-overlap condition is used.

statements.statement: Let b_q(x)=E_P[Gamma_q(O;etahat)|X=x]-tau_P(x), with cross-fitted nuisances fixed on the evaluation fold. For gamma>0, any pi with r=R_P(pi) satisfies |P[(pi-pi_star)b_q]| <= C{r_mu_n r_e_n/q + r_mu_n u^{alpha/2}q^{1/(2 gamma)} + r_mu_n(r/u)^{1/2}} whenever q<=c_o u^gamma. For gamma=0 and fixed q<=underline_p/2, |P[(pi-pi_star)b_q]| <= C r_mu_n r_e_n.
statements.proof_tex: Fix the training folds and write Delta_a=muhat_a-mu_a and b_q(x)=E_P[Gamma_q(O;etahat)|X=x]-tau_P(x). Lem:clip-bias gives b_q=(ehat_q-e_P){Delta_1/ehat_q+Delta_0/(1-ehat_q)}. Since the clipped denominators are at least q, |b_q|<=q^{-1}|ehat_q-e_P|(|Delta_1|+|Delta_0|). Let I_q=[q,1-q] and Pi_q be projection onto I_q. Then ehat_q=Pi_q(ehat) and |ehat_q-e_P|<=|ehat-e_P|+dist(e_P,I_q)<=|ehat-e_P|+q 1{p_P<=q}. For any policy pi with regret r, the deterministic drift contribution is bounded by E[1_{D_pi}|b_q|]. The first part is at most q^{-1} sum_a E[|ehat-e_P||Delta_a|]<=C r_mu_n r_e_n/q by Cauchy-Schwarz and ass:nuisance-rate. For gamma>0, the second part is at most sum_a E[1_{D_pi}1{p_P<=q}|Delta_a|]<=C r_mu_n P_X(D_pi intersect {p_P<=q})^{1/2}. Applying lem:clipped-region-localization and sqrt{x+y}<=sqrt{x}+sqrt{y} gives C r_mu_n{u^{alpha/2}q^{1/(2 gamma)}+(r/u)^{1/2}} whenever q<=c_o u^gamma. This proves the gamma>0 bound. When gamma=0 and q<=underline_p/2, ass:strict-overlap-endpoint implies e_P in I_q almost surely, so dist(e_P,I_q)=0 and |ehat_q-e_P|<=|ehat-e_P|. The same Cauchy-Schwarz step gives E[1_{D_pi}|b_q|]<=C r_mu_n r_e_n, with the fixed q absorbed into C.

statements.statement: Under ass:bounded-outcome and ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, for every q in (0,1/2] and every cross-fitted nuisance triple used in def:feasible-erm, the clipped AIPW score satisfies |Gamma_q(O;etahat)|<=C/q almost surely and hence E[Gamma_q(O;etahat)^2 | X]<=C/q^2.
statements.proof_tex: By def:clipped-propensity, the clipped denominator e_q lies in [q,1-q], so both e_q and 1-e_q are at least q. By ass:bounded-outcome and ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, |Y-muhat_a(X)|<=2 and |muhat_1(X)-muhat_0(X)|<=2. In def:clipped-aipw-score only one of the inverse-propensity residual terms is active because A is binary. Therefore |Gamma_q(O;etahat)|<=2+2/q<=C/q for q<=1/2. Squaring gives the conditional second-moment bound.

statements.statement: For gamma>0, under ass:overlap-decay and ass:zero-effect, every policy pi with regret r=R_P(pi) satisfies P_X(D_pi intersect {p_P<=q}) <= C u^alpha q^{1/gamma}+r/u whenever 0<u<=u_0 and q<=c_o u^gamma.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Let D=D_pi. By ass:zero-effect, the portion of D on {tau_P=0} has P_X-measure zero. Hence, up to a null set,
D intersect {p_P<=q} subset {p_P<=q, 0<|tau_P|<=u} union (D intersect {|tau_P|>u}).
The first set has probability at most C_o u^alpha q^{1/gamma} by ass:overlap-decay because q<=c_o u^gamma. For the second set, thm:welfare-identity gives r=int_D |tau_P| dP_X, so P_X(D intersect {|tau_P|>u})<=r/u. Adding the two bounds proves the claim.

statements.statement: For the deterministic schedule in def:feasible-rate, the terms in lem:crude-localized-master-bound optimize to the exponent r_up=r_feas. For gamma>0, with A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha), q_n=q_0 n^{-s_feas}, u_n=u_bar n^{-t_feas}, and g_joint=max_{0<=s<=1/2, 0<=t<=s/gamma} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)+alpha t/2, 2a-t}, the master bound is at most C{n^{-r_star}+n^{-g_joint}}(log n)^p = C n^{-min{r_star,g_joint}}(log n)^p. For gamma=0, the exponent is min{A_alpha,c}.
statements.proof_tex:
Proof. Let \(A_\alpha=(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha)\). Suppose first that \(\gamma>0\). For a feasible schedule \(q_n=q_0n^{-s}\), \(u_n=\bar u n^{-t}\) with \(0\le s\le1/2\) and \(0\le t\le s/\gamma\), the normalization in def:feasible-rate gives \(q_n\le c_ou_n^\gamma\) for all large \(n\). The four non-oracle terms in lem:crude-localized-master-bound have exponents
\[
(nq_n^2)^{-A_\alpha}=C n^{-A_\alpha(1-2s)},
\]
\[
r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}/q_n\le Cn^{-(c-s)},
\]
\[
r_{\mu,n}u_n^{\alpha/2}q_n^{1/(2\gamma)}\le Cn^{-\{a+s/(2\gamma)+\alpha t/2\}},
\]
and
\[
r_{\mu,n}^2/u_n\le Cn^{-(2a-t)}.
\]
The last three displays use ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents. Thus, for this \((s,t)\), the feasible part of the master bound is at most \(Cn^{-\phi(s,t)}\), where
\[
\phi(s,t)=\min\{A_\alpha(1-2s),c-s,a+s/(2\gamma)+\alpha t/2,2a-t\}.
\]
The function \(\phi\) is continuous on the compact feasible set, so the maximizer chosen in def:feasible-rate exists and has value \(g_{joint}\). Consequently the gamma-positive upper exponent is
\[
r_{up}=r_{feas}=\min\{r_\star(\alpha,\gamma),g_{joint}(\alpha,\gamma,a,c)\}.
\]
If \(\gamma=0\), the clip is fixed, so the stochastic term has exponent \(A_\alpha\), and ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents gives the product-nuisance exponent \(c\). Therefore \(r_{up}=r_{feas}=\min\{A_\alpha,c\}\).

sampling_model.design: i.i.d. offline (X,A,Y) from P; binary A; bounded Y in [-1,1]; n observations.

estimand_functional: R_P(pi) = V_P(pi_star) - V_P(pi), with V_P(pi)=E_P[pi(X) tau_P(X)]; target = minimax regret rate.
target_estimand: Minimax welfare-regret rate M_n(alpha,gamma) = inf_{pi_hat} sup_{P in P_class(alpha,gamma)} E_P R_P(pi_hat), with R_P(pi) = V_P(pi_star) - V_P(pi).

Kitagawa, T., and Tetenov, A. (2018). Who should be treated? Empirical welfare maximization methods for treatment choice. Econometrica 86(2), 591-616.
Tsybakov, A. B. (2004). Optimal aggregation of classifiers in statistical learning. Annals of Statistics 32(1), 135-166.
Audibert, J.-Y., and Tsybakov, A. B. (2007). Fast learning rates for plug-in classifiers. Annals of Statistics 35(2), 608-633.
van der Vaart, A. W., and Wellner, J. A. (1996). Weak Convergence and Empirical Processes. Springer.
Bartlett, P. L., Bousquet, O., and Mendelson, S. (2005). Local Rademacher complexities. Annals of Statistics 33(4), 1497-1537.
Imbens, G. W., and Rubin, D. B. (2015). Causal Inference for Statistics, Social, and Biomedical Sciences. Cambridge University Press.

tldr: Welfare-regret rate for offline policy learning over an observed-law class with coupled Tsybakov-margin (alpha) and one-sided polynomial overlap-decay (gamma). The note's headline result is the explicit converse n^{-r_*(alpha,gamma)}, r_*=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}), proved by an EXPLICIT two-point construction (no assumed least-favorable family). Separately, a feasible cross-fit clipped-AIPW ERM attains the conditional rate n^{-r_feas}, r_feas=min{r_*, g(a,c,gamma)} on the explicit nuisance/fold/offset side-condition domain of def:upper-risk; the strict-gap tightness question remains open.

project_justification.gap: Offline policy-learning minimax regret is characterized under strict overlap (n^{-1/2}, Athey-Wager) and under margin alone (Audibert-Tsybakov), but not under coupled margin and one-sided polynomial overlap decay, where the propensity vanishes near the decision boundary.
project_justification.niche: A single exponent r_*(alpha,gamma) governing the regret floor when margin mass and overlap decay are tied through one envelope; the weak-overlap obstruction that makes ATE estimation irregular (Khan-Tamer) does NOT bind the margin-localized regret loss.
project_justification.fill: An explicit two-point converse M_n >= c n^{-r_*} over the observed-law class, and a feasible cross-fit clipped-AIPW ERM upper bound n^{-r_feas}, r_feas=min{r_*, g}, on that same baseline class plus explicit nuisance/fold/offset side conditions; matched when nuisance rates suffice, with an honest weak-overlap gap r_*-g and an open tightness question (oeq:feasible-tight) otherwise.

related_work: Athey-Wager (2021) and Kitagawa-Tetenov (2018) establish strict-overlap policy-learning minimax regret and VC achievability; Tsybakov (2004) and Audibert-Tsybakov (2007) the margin-condition converse machinery; Khan-Tamer (2010) the irregular-identification obstruction for ATE under limited overlap (which this paper shows does not transfer to regret); Luedtke-Chambaz (2017) margin-driven faster policy rates.
honest_scope: The paper headline is the sharp constructive minimax lower bound over the baseline observed-law class. The feasible clipped-AIPW result is an honest conditional achievability theorem for a supplied cross-fitted nuisance learner satisfying explicit nuisance, fixed-K cross-fitting, and localized offset side conditions; it uses only the crude q^{-2} score envelope and deterministic clip-bias from overlap-decay/margin. Consistency and unconfoundedness remain optional causal semantics for interpreting the observed-law objects, but they are not load-bearing in the stated rate class. No matched feasible minimax rate is asserted in the strict-gap branch; oeq:feasible-tight records that residual.
